mission: Freeze the current GP principal-system provider path strategy as a split between mock-now executable truth, official watch-only lanes, and gated actual-provider-later routes without letting supplier folklore bypass the canonical booking proof model.

assumptions.summary: Because current official GP Connect materials describe Appointment Management as live but say new supplier development is paused, Vecells records GP Connect Appointment Management as a watch-only estate path rather than a current baseline build target.
assumptions.consequence: The programme can preserve awareness of real estates without silently re-basing the product on GP Connect.

assumptions.summary: Official guidance positions BaRS in broader referral and urgent-care scenarios. Vecells therefore keeps BaRS visible as a watch rail while refusing to treat it as the current principal-GP-system patient-booking path.
assumptions.consequence: Cross-provider and urgent-care compatibility stays visible without diluting the principal-system booking truth model.

assumptions.summary: The IM1 pages and blueprint both say detailed supplier capability arrives later. Seq_036 therefore freezes only what is current and public, and leaves hold semantics, manage coverage, and exact slot behaviour under later PIP evidence.
assumptions.consequence: The matrix stays honest about what is known today and pushes missing detail into the gap register instead of inventing supplier truth.

capability_dimensions.title: Patient booking search
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Patient search cannot be inferred from staff or API capability claims.

capability_dimensions.title: Staff-assisted search
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Staff-assisted routes may widen actionability without unlocking patient self-service.

capability_dimensions.title: Slot freshness proof
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Showing a slot and proving it remained valid at commit time are different facts.

capability_dimensions.title: Hold or soft-hold support
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Exclusive hold, truthful non-exclusive hold, and no hold are different patient promises.

capability_dimensions.title: Authoritative commit proof
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Accepted, queued, and confirmed outcomes must not collapse into one state.

capability_dimensions.title: Cancellation support
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Cancel may exist even when reschedule or detail update do not.

capability_dimensions.title: Reschedule support
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Reschedule requires stronger tuple and revalidation guarantees than basic cancel.

capability_dimensions.title: Reminder or detail update support
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Reminder readiness flows from confirmed booking truth and manage capability, not from search support.

capability_dimensions.title: Waitlist continuation compatibility
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Local waitlist continuation must stay core-owned even when supplier search or commit differs.

capability_dimensions.title: Practice-visibility evidence
capability_dimensions.why_separate: The practice or origin service may need a separate acknowledgement trail from patient confirmation.

capability_dimensions.title: Ambiguity mode
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Async acceptance, disputed callbacks, and partial evidence must stay visibly pending.

capability_dimensions.title: Degraded fallback mode
capability_dimensions.why_separate: Fallback must be explicit and same-shell, not a hidden operational assumption.

capability_dimensions.title: Exact proof object required before reassurance
capability_dimensions.why_separate: The UI must bind to a named object chain, not generic success language.

canonical_proof_objects.role: Static capability source row for the exact supplier, audience, route, and deployment context.
The only legal translation and proof contract for search, revalidation, reservation, commit, and manage support.
The audience-specific actionability decision for the current tuple and binding hash.
Truth for exclusive hold, truthful non-exclusive posture, or released state before commit.
Mandatory external truth gate whenever supplier confirmation is weak, async, manual, or disputed.
Durable booking record only after authoritative success under the active binding.
The sole audience-safe authority for booked reassurance and writable manage posture.
The practice-visibility proof object when origin services need explicit acknowledgement beyond patient confirmation.

proof_ladders.title: IM1 authoritative commit or read-after-write proof
steps: Resolve one current ProviderCapabilityMatrix row and one current BookingProviderAdapterBinding for the exact tuple.
Persist BookingCapabilityResolution and revalidate slot freshness against the same tuple and policy envelope.
Observe durable provider reference or same-commit read-after-write under the active binding.
Create AppointmentRecord on the same BookingTransaction lineage.
Refresh BookingConfirmationTruthProjection to confirmed before any booked reassurance or writable manage posture.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: Booked summary appears only after step 5.

proof_ladders.title: Watch-only consumer estate proof
steps: Treat the route as an external watch lane, not a current executable baseline.
Require estate-specific supplier evidence before any capability claim is promoted.
If adopted later, normalize every success or pending state into the same canonical truth objects as IM1.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: No current patient reassurance is allowed from this lane.

proof_ladders.title: BaRS inter-provider proof boundary
steps: Keep BaRS classified as a broader referral interoperability rail.
Require a future bounded use-case decision before any appointment-control claim is made.
If a later booking use case emerges, bind it through the same canonical proof objects and ExternalConfirmationGate rules.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: BaRS does not currently unlock principal-GP booked reassurance.

proof_ladders.title: Simulator proof ladder
steps: Compile the current BookingProviderAdapterBinding and BookingCapabilityResolution under deterministic fixtures.
Simulate slot search, revalidation, exclusive or truthful reservation semantics, and commit attempts.
Simulate both authoritative success and ambiguous or disputed outcomes under ExternalConfirmationGate.
Materialize AppointmentRecord and confirmed BookingConfirmationTruthProjection only in the success branch.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: The simulator may render every state, but it must obey the same proof law as live paths.

proof_ladders.title: Manual handoff acknowledgement ladder
steps: Create manual follow-up or callback obligation instead of pretending slot or hold truth exists.
Capture explicit practice acknowledgement or fallback transfer evidence.
Keep ExternalConfirmationGate open until manual confirmation settles or recovery wins.
Refresh BookingConfirmationTruthProjection only after the manual outcome becomes canonical truth.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: Manual contact never implies immediate booked calmness.

dimension_digest.slot_freshness_proof: Current revalidation through BookingProviderAdapterBinding under the live capability tuple.
dimension_digest.hold_or_soft_hold_support: Unknown until supplier PIP and capability row are reviewed; never promise exclusivity by default.
dimension_digest.authoritative_commit_proof: Durable provider reference or same-commit read-after-write on the same BookingTransaction.

path_requirements.exact_proof_object_required: BookingConfirmationTruthProjection.confirmed plus AppointmentRecord on the same transaction, backed by the current BookingProviderAdapterBinding and BookingCapabilityResolution.

dimension_digest.slot_freshness_proof: Current revalidation through BookingProviderAdapterBinding under the live capability tuple.
dimension_digest.hold_or_soft_hold_support: Unknown until supplier PIP and capability row are reviewed; never promise exclusivity by default.
dimension_digest.authoritative_commit_proof: Durable provider reference or same-commit read-after-write on the same BookingTransaction.

path_requirements.exact_proof_object_required: BookingConfirmationTruthProjection.confirmed plus AppointmentRecord on the same transaction, backed by the current BookingProviderAdapterBinding and BookingCapabilityResolution.

path_requirements.path_label: GP Connect Appointment Management / Watch only

dimension_digest.slot_freshness_proof: Consumer-supplier specific and not current Vecells baseline evidence.
dimension_digest.authoritative_commit_proof: Do not claim without estate-specific supplier evidence and normalized truth mapping.

path_requirements.exact_proof_object_required: Same BookingConfirmationTruthProjection and AppointmentRecord chain as IM1; GP Connect does not bypass that.

dimension_digest.slot_freshness_proof: Standard-level interoperability does not replace supplier-specific local slot freshness proof.
dimension_digest.authoritative_commit_proof: Useful for referral or booking exchange, but not sufficient alone for principal-GP-system booked reassurance.

path_requirements.exact_proof_object_required: If ever used for booking confirmation, the same BookingConfirmationTruthProjection chain still applies.

dimension_digest.slot_freshness_proof: Snapshot plus revalidation contract under deterministic state injection.
dimension_digest.hold_or_soft_hold_support: exclusive_hold and truthful_nonexclusive both simulated explicitly.
dimension_digest.authoritative_commit_proof: Durable reference, same-commit read-after-write, ambiguous acceptance, and failure paths simulated.

path_requirements.exact_proof_object_required: Same canonical proof chain as live: BookingProviderAdapterBinding, BookingCapabilityResolution, ExternalConfirmationGate when needed, AppointmentRecord, and BookingConfirmationTruthProjection.

dimension_digest.slot_freshness_proof: No authoritative portal slot-freshness claim; practice confirmation required.
dimension_digest.authoritative_commit_proof: Manual confirmation only after explicit practice acknowledgement and canonical projection refresh.

path_requirements.exact_proof_object_required: ExternalConfirmationGate satisfied plus current BookingConfirmationTruthProjection for the same lineage.

live_gate_digest.title: Architecture and data-flow diagrams are current
Sponsor and commissioning posture is explicit
Named approver and environment are present
No unresolved blocker remains in the watch register
Phase 0 external foundation gate is open

official_guidance.title: IM1 Pairing integration
official_guidance.summary: NHS England still positions IM1 Pairing as the current principal-system route. The page names Optum (EMISWeb) and TPP (SystmOne), says there are no current plans to decommission IM1 Pairing, and shows the path from prerequisites through SCAL, supported test, assurance, and live rollout.
grounding: Optum (EMISWeb) and TPP (SystmOne) are the currently named provider suppliers.
IM1 Pairing has no current decommission plan.
Supplier-specific functionality differs and detailed technical packs arrive later through PIP acceptance.
The route remains long-lead and assurance-heavy rather than instantly usable.

official_guidance.title: GP Connect Appointment Management
official_guidance.summary: The GP Connect Appointment Management page describes the API as live and highlights its primary use in integrated urgent care, care navigation, and care coordination. It also names current embedded consumer suppliers instead of inviting new baseline product onboarding.
grounding: The page describes care navigation, care coordination, and integrated urgent care use cases.
Approved consumer suppliers are listed in current embedded estates rather than described as an open baseline expansion lane.
The examples are staff and service-coordination oriented, not Vecells patient-shell self-service truth.

official_guidance.title: GP Connect supplier progress
official_guidance.summary: The supplier-progress page explicitly says new supplier development for Appointment Management is currently paused while existing consumer suppliers remain listed.
grounding: Appointment Management new supplier development is currently paused.
Consumer estates are current but bounded to listed suppliers and use cases.

official_guidance.title: Guidance for referrals and bookings specific use cases
official_guidance.summary: NHS England's guidance explicitly says patient-to-GP-practice booking applications are generally integrated with principal clinical systems through IM1 Pairing Patient APIs, while BaRS is presented in referral and urgent-care use cases.
grounding: Patient-to-GP-practice booking is generally integrated through IM1 Pairing Patient APIs.
BaRS appears alongside urgent care and inter-service referrals rather than as a principal GP-system route.
The guidance helps separate principal-system booking from broader referral interoperability.

official_guidance.title: NHS Booking and Referral Standard
official_guidance.summary: The standard defines BaRS as a FHIR-based interoperability standard between patient record systems for booking and referral information exchange. That is important, but it still does not make BaRS the current Vecells baseline for principal-GP-system patient self-service booking.
grounding: BaRS is a standard for interoperability between patient record systems.
The standard is broader than one GP principal system estate and needs bounded interpretation inside Vecells.
